rqs: What are the time and space complexities of QuickSort, Merge Sort, and Radix Sort compared to Bubble, Insertion, and Heap Sort in academic journal studies?
Which parallel or GPU sorting algorithms provide the best scalability for high‑performance computing and embedded systems?
In which contexts (benchmark suites, open‑source repositories) do QuickSort and Merge Sort outperform traditional sorts in runtime and throughput?
base_query: ("Software Developers" OR "Algorithm Researchers" OR "Data Processing Systems" OR "Embedded Systems" OR "High Performance Computing" OR "researcher") AND ("QuickSort" OR "Merge Sort" OR "Radix Sort" OR "Parallel Sorting" OR "GPU Sorting") AND ("Bubble Sort" OR "Insertion Sort" OR "Heap Sort" OR "Selection Sort" OR "Shell Sort") AND ("Time Complexity" OR "Space Complexity" OR "Runtime" OR "Throughput" OR "Scalability") AND ("Academic Journals" OR "Conference Proceedings" OR "Open Source Repositories" OR "Benchmark Suites" OR "Industry Reports")
